subject: Public Health
title: The Polio Vaccine Race
description: Learn The Polio Vaccine Race in one minute.

hook.text: In the 1950s, parents lived in terror of summer—polio season paralyzed thousands of children each year.

buildup.text: Jonas Salk developed an inactivated polio vaccine in 1955. Albert Sabin created an oral version in 1961.

discovery.text: Salk's vaccine used killed virus. Sabin's used a weakened live virus—easier to give and spread immunity.

twist.text: When asked who owned the patent, Salk replied: 'The people. Could you patent the sun?'

climax.text: Polio cases dropped from 350,000 per year to just 12 in 2023—eradication is within reach.

punchline.text: Salk gave the vaccine to the world. He gave the patent to no one.

quiz.question: What did Jonas Salk say about patenting the polio vaccine?
options: 'Could you patent the sun?' — he gave it freely
He sold the patent for $1 million
He licensed it exclusively to one company
